name: N

unions.name: BelowOrAbove
unions.comment:
The error result of comparing [`N`](#N)s.

  - `Above`: greater
  - `Below`: less

Values exist for each condition.

unions.name: Diff
unions.comment:
`Diff low To high`: an exact number as the difference `high - low`.

    In
        N5
        (Add5 a_)
        (Is
            (Diff myAge To sistersAge)
            (Diff mothersAge To fathersAge)
        )

  - `myAge + 5 = sistersAge`
  - `mothersAge + 5 = fathersAge`
cases: { add : low -> high, sub : high -> low }

unions.name: N0able
unions.comment:
Base type of [`N0`](#N0), [`Add1 n`](#Add1) following [`allowable-state`](https://dark.elm.dmy.fr/packages/lue-bird/elm-allowable-state/latest/):

Is the parameter `possiblyOrNever` set to `Never` like in

    type alias Add1 n =
        N0able n Never

`N0` is impossible to construct.

Is the parameter `possiblyOrNever` set to `Possibly` like in

    type alias N0 =
        N0able Never Possibly

`N0` is a possible value.

unions.name: To
unions.comment:
Just a word in the [type `Diff`](#Diff).

    Diff low To high

→ distance `high - low`.

aliases.name: Exactly
aliases.comment:
Allow only a specific number.

Useful as a **stored & argument** type in combination with [`typesafe-array`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-typesafe-array/latest/)s,
not with [`N`](#N)s.

    byte : Arr (Exactly N8) Bit -> Byte

→ A given [`Arr`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-typesafe-array/latest/) must have _exactly 8_ `Bit`s.

    type alias TicTacToeBoard =
        Arr
            (Exactly N3)
            (Arr (Exactly N3) TicTacToField)

→ A given [`Arr`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-typesafe-array/latest/) must have _exactly 3 by 3_ `TicTacToeField`s.
aliases.type: N.In n n {}

aliases.name: In
aliases.comment:
somewhere within a `minimum` & `maximum`

       ↓ minimum   ↓ maximum
    ⨯ [✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓] ⨯ ⨯ ⨯...


### `In minimum maximum difference_`

For arguments

    percent : N (In min_ N100 difference_) -> Percent

→ `min ≤ N100`

If you want a number where you just care about the minimum, leave the `max` as a type _variable_.

       ↓ minimum    ↓ maximum or  →
    ⨯ [✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓...

Any natural number:

    N (In min_ max_ difference_)

A number, at least 5:

    N (In (Add5 minMinus5_) max_ difference_)

  - `max_` could be a specific maximum or [no maximum at all](#NoMax)

  - `difference_` could contain extra information if the argument is a [specific](#Is) number
    like [`n0`](#n0), [`n1`](#n1), ...


### `In minimum maximum {}`

A number somewhere within a `minimum` & `maximum`. We don't know it exactly, though

       ↓ minimum   ↓ maximum
    ⨯ [✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓] ⨯ ⨯ ⨯...

Do **not** use it as an argument type.

A number between 3 and 5

    N (In N3 (Add5 a_) {})
aliases.type: RecordWithoutConstructorFunction.RecordWithoutConstructorFunction { min : minimum, max : () -> maximum, diff : possibleRepresentationAsDifferences }

aliases.name: Is
aliases.comment: Associated [difference](#Diff)s.
aliases.type: RecordWithoutConstructorFunction.RecordWithoutConstructorFunction { diff0 : difference0, diff1 : difference1 }

aliases.name: Min
aliases.comment:
Only **stored / result types should be `Min`**.

Sometimes, you simply cannot compute a maximum.

    abs : Int -> N (In N0 ?? {})
                    ↓
    abs : Int -> N (Min N0)

A number `≥ 5` for example:

    N (Min N5)

Every `Min min` is of type `In min ...`.
aliases.type: N.In lowestPossibleValue N.NoMax {}

aliases.name: N
aliases.comment:
A **bounded** natural number `>= 0`


### argument type

    -- ≥ 0, else any limitations allowed
    N range_

    -- ≥ 4
    N (In (Add4 minMinus4_) max_ difference_)

    -- 4 ≤ n ≤ 15
    N (In (Add4 minMinus4_) N15 difference_)

`In (Add4 minMinus4_) N15` says:

  - the minimum-constraint can be `4`|`5`|`6`|...
  - any maximum-constraint greater than `15` is forbidden
  - specific numbers like [`n0`](#n0), [`n1`](#n1), ... which carry [`difference_`](#Is)s are allowed


### result type

    -- ≥ 4
    N (Min N4)

    -- 2 ≤ n ≤ 12
    N (In N2 (Add12 a_) {})

`In N2 (Add12 a_) {}` says:

  - the minimum-constraint can be `4`|`5`|`6`|...
  - any maximum-constraint greater than `15` is forbidden
  - `{}`: no [differences](#Is) are attached.
    Only specific numbers like [`n0`](#n0), [`n1`](#n1), ... carry [differences](#Is)s


### stored type

what to put in declared types like `Model`

    -- ≥ 4
    N (Min N4)

    -- 2 ≤ n ≤ 12
    N (In N2 N12 {})

They are like [result types](#result-type) but without type variables.


### specific number

Numbers [`n0`](#n0), [`n1`](#n1), ... supplied by this library.

The type [`Is`](#Is) `(`[`Diff x0 To nPlusX0`](#Diff)`) ...`
enables adding, subtracting `N<x>` types.
Consider the type an implementation detail.
You can come back to [understand them later](#internals).

    n3 :
        N
            (In
                N3
                (Add3 atLeast_)
                (Is
                    (Diff x0 To (Add3 x0))
                    (Diff x1 To (Add3 x1))
                )
            )

    -- a number nTo15 away from 15
    N (In n nAtLeast_ (Is (Diff nTo15 To N15) diff1_))
aliases.type: N.Internal.N range

aliases.name: NoMax
aliases.comment:
Flag "The number's maximum limit is unknown".

    type alias Min min =
        In minimum NoMax {}

is the definition of [`Min`](#Min).

An example where this is useful using [typesafe-array](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-typesafe-array/latest/):

    type Tree childCountMax element
        = Tree
            element
            (Arr
                (In N0 childCountMax {})
                (Maybe (Tree childCountMax element))
            )

    type alias TreeBinary element =
        Tree N2 element

    type alias TreeMulti element =
        Tree NoMax element

Remember: ↑ and other [`Min`](#Min)/[`NoMax`](#NoMax) are result/stored types, not argument types.
You can just use a variable `Tree childCountMax_ element` if you don't care about an enforced maximum.
aliases.type: N.Internal.NoMax

values.name: abs
values.comment:
The absolute value of an `Int` → `≥ 0`

    -4 |> N.abs |> N.toInt --> 4

    16 |> N.abs |> N.toInt --> 16

Really only use this if you want the absolute value.

    badLength =
        List.length >> N.abs

  - maybe, there's a solution that never even theoretically deals with unexpected values:

        mostCorrectLength =
            List.foldl
                (\_ -> N.minAdd n1 >> N.minDown n0)
                (n0 |> N.noMax)

  - other times, though, like with `Array.length`, which isn't `O(n)`,
    you can escape with for example

        arrayLength =
            Array.length >> N.intAtLeast n0
values.type: Basics.Int -> N.N (N.Min N.N0)

values.name: add
values.comment:
Add a given specific [`N (In ... (Is ...))`](#Is).

    between70And100 |> N.add n7
    --: N (In N77 (Add107 a_) {})

Use [`addIn`](#addIn) if you want to add an [`N`](#N) that isn't a [`N (In ... (Is ...))`](#Is).
values.type: N.N (N.In added_ addedAtLeast_ (N.Is (N.Diff min N.To sumMin) (N.Diff max N.To sumMax))) -> N.N (N.In min max difference_) -> N.N (N.In sumMin sumMax {})

values.name: addAtLeast
values.comment:
To the [`N`](#N) without a known maximum-constraint,,
add an [`N`](#N). The second argument is the minimum added value.

    atLeast5 |> N.addAtLeast n2 atLeast2
    --: N (Min N7)

Use [`minAdd`](#minAdd) to add exact numbers.
values.type: N.N (N.In addedMin addedMinAtLeast_ (N.Is (N.Diff min N.To sumMin) addedDiff1_)) -> N.N (N.In addedMin addedMax_ addedDifference_) -> N.N (N.In min max_ difference_) -> N.N (N.Min sumMin)

values.name: addDifference
values.comment:
To the number, add a specific other one by supplying one of its [difference](#Diff)s
promised by its type [`Is (Diff low0 To high0) (Diff low1 To high1)`](#Is)
values.type: N.Diff low N.To high -> low -> high

values.name: addIn
values.comment:
Add an [`N`](#N) that can be in a range.

The tuple argument should contain

1.  the minimum added value
2.  the maximum added value

```
between3And10 |> N.addIn ( n1, n12 ) between1And12
--: N (In N4 (N22Plus a_) {})
```
values.type: ( N.N (N.In addedMin addedMinAtLeast_ (N.Is (N.Diff min N.To sumMin) addedMinDiff1_)), N.N (N.In addedMax addedMaxAtLeast_ (N.Is (N.Diff max N.To sumMax) addedMaxDiff1_)) ) -> N.N (N.In addedMin addedMax addedDifference_) -> N.N (N.In min max difference_) -> N.N (N.In sumMin sumMax {})

values.name: atLeast
values.comment:
Return the given number if the [`N`](#N) is less.

    between5And9 |> N.atLeast n10
    --: N (In N10 (Add10 a_) {})

    n15 |> N.atLeast n10 |> N.toInt
    --> 15

    n5AtLeast |> N.atLeast (n10 |> noMax)
    --: N (Min N10)

(The type doesn't forbid that the lower limit you're comparing against
is below the current minimum.)
values.type: N.N (N.In minNewMin (N.Add1 maxMinus1) lowerDifference_) -> N.N (N.In min_ (N.Add1 maxMinus1) difference_) -> N.N (N.In minNewMin (N.Add1 maxMinus1) {})

values.name: atMost
values.comment:
**Cap** the [`N`](#N) to at most a number.

    between5And15
        |> N.atMost n10
    --: N (In N5 (Add10 a_) {})

    atLeast5 |> N.atMost n10
    --: N (In N5 (Add10 a_) {})

(The type doesn't forbid that the upper limit you're comparing against
is above the current maximum.)
values.type: N.N (N.In min maxCapped capDifference_) -> N.N (N.In min max_ difference_) -> N.N (N.In min maxCapped {})

values.name: diffAdd
values.comment:
The [`N (In ... (Is ...))`](#Is) plus another [`N (In ... (Is ...))`](#Is). Give the added value twice as a tuple.

    n6 |> N.diffAdd ( n5, n5 )
    --→ n11
    --: N
    --:     (In
    --:         N11
    --:         (Add11 a_)
    --:         (Is
    --:             (Diff x0 To (Add11 x0))
    --:             (Diff x1 To (Add11 x1))
    --:         )
    --:     )

This is only rarely useful, as for example

    isInRangeXToXPlus10 x =
        isIn ( x, x |> N.diffAdd ( n10, n10 ) )

would force x to be of type N (In ... (Is ...)). Instead,

    isInXToXPlus10 x =
        isIn ( x, x |> N.add n10 )
values.type: ( N.N (N.In added added0AtLeast_ (N.Is (N.Diff n N.To sum) (N.Diff nAtLeast N.To sumAtLeast))), N.N (N.In added added1AtLeast_ (N.Is (N.Diff x0PlusN N.To aPlusSum) (N.Diff x1PlusN N.To bPlusSum))) ) -> N.N (N.In n nAtLeast (N.Is (N.Diff x0 N.To x0PlusN) (N.Diff x1 N.To x1PlusN))) -> N.N (N.In sum sumAtLeast (N.Is (N.Diff x0 N.To aPlusSum) (N.Diff x1 N.To bPlusSum)))

values.name: diffSub
values.comment:
The `N (In ... (Is ...)` minus another [`N (In ... (Is ...))`](#Is). Give the subtracted value twice as a tuple.

    n6 |> N.diffSub ( n5, n5 )
    --→ n1
    --: N
    --:     (In
    --:         N1
    --:         (Add1 a_)
    --:         (Is
    --:             (Diff x0 To (Add1 x0))
    --:             (Diff x1 To (Add1 x1))
    --:         )
    --:     )

This is only rarely useful, as for example

    isInXMinus10ToX x =
        isIn ( x |> N.diffSub ( n10, n10 ), x )

would force `x` to be of type [`N (In ... (Is ...))`](#Is).
Instead,

    isInXMinus10ToX x =
        isIn ( x |> N.sub n10, x )
values.type: ( N.N (N.In subtracted subtracted0AtLeast_ (N.Is (N.Diff difference N.To n) (N.Diff differenceAtLeast N.To nAtLeast))), N.N (N.In subtracted subtracted1AtLeast_ (N.Is (N.Diff x0PlusDifference N.To x0PlusN) (N.Diff x1PlusDifference N.To x1PlusN))) ) -> N.N (N.In n nAtLeast (N.Is (N.Diff x0 N.To x0PlusN) (N.Diff x1 N.To x1PlusN))) -> N.N (N.In difference differenceAtLeast (N.Is (N.Diff x0 N.To x0PlusDifference) (N.Diff x1 N.To x1PlusDifference)))

values.name: difference0
values.comment: The number representation as a [difference](#Diff) promised by its type [`Is (Diff low To high) ...`](#Is).
values.type: N.N (N.In min_ max_ { differences_ | diff0 : difference0 }) -> difference0

values.name: difference1
values.comment: The number representation as a [difference](#Diff) promised by its type [`Is ... (Diff low To high)`](#Is).
values.type: N.N (N.In min_ max_ { differences_ | diff1 : difference1 }) -> difference1

values.name: differenceAdd
values.comment: Chain the [difference](#Diff) further to a higher number.
values.type: N.Diff middle N.To high -> N.Diff low N.To middle -> N.Diff low N.To high

values.name: differenceSub
values.comment: Chain the [difference](#Diff) back to a lower number.
values.type: N.Diff middle N.To high -> N.Diff low N.To high -> N.Diff low N.To middle

values.name: differences
values.comment:
The number representation as all [difference](#Diff)s promised by its type [`Is (Diff low0 To high0) (Diff low1 To high1)`](#Is).

For the individual [difference](#Diff)s: [`difference0`](#difference0), [`difference1`](#difference1)
values.type: N.N (N.In min_ max_ differences) -> differences

values.name: div
values.comment:
Divide (`//`) by an [`N`](#N) `d ≥ 1`.

  - `/ 0` is impossible
  - `x / d` is at most x

```
atMost7
    |> N.div n3
    --: N (In N0 (Add7 a_) {})
    |> N.toInt
--> 2
```
values.type: N.N (N.In (N.Add1 divisorMinMinus1_) divisorMax_ divisorDifference_) -> N.N (N.In min_ max difference_) -> N.N (N.In N.N0 max {})

values.name: intAtLeast
values.comment:
A `N (Min ...)` from an `Int`; if the `Int < minimum`, `minimum` is returned.

    0
        |> N.intAtLeast n3
        --: N (Min N3)
        |> N.toInt
    --> 3

    9
        |> N.intAtLeast n3
        --: N (Min N3)
        |> N.toInt
    --> 9

You can also use this if you know an `Int` must be at least `minimum`.

But avoid it if you can do better, like

    goodLength =
        List.foldl
            (\_ -> N.minAdd n1 >> N.minDown n0)
            (n0 |> N.noMax)

If you want to handle the case `< minimum` yourself → [`intIsAtLeast`](#intIsAtLeast)
values.type: N.N (N.In min max_ lowerDifference_) -> Basics.Int -> N.N (N.Min min)

values.name: intIn
values.comment:
Create a `N (In ...)` by **clamping** an `Int` between a minimum & maximum.

  - if the `Int < minimum`, `minimum` is returned
  - if the `Int > maximum`, `maximum` is returned

If you want to handle the cases `< minimum` & `> maximum` explicitly, use [`intIsIn`](#intIsIn).

    0
        |> N.intIn ( n3, n12 )
        --: N (In N3 (Add12 a_) {})
        |> N.toInt
    --> 3

    99
        |> N.intIn ( n3, n12 )
        --: N (In N3 (Add12 a_) {})
        |> N.toInt
    --> 12

    9
        |> N.intIn ( n3, n12 )
        --: N (In N3 (Add12 a_) {})
        |> N.toInt
    --> 9


    toDigit : Char -> Maybe (N (In N0 (Add9 atLeast_) {}))
    toDigit char =
        ((char |> Char.toCode) - ('0' |> Char.toCode))
            |> N.intIsIn ( n0, n9 )
            |> Result.toMaybe
values.type: ( N.N (N.In lowerLimit upperLimitMin lowerLimitDifference_), N.N (N.In upperLimitMin upperLimit upperLimitDifference_) ) -> Basics.Int -> N.N (N.In lowerLimit upperLimit {})

values.name: intIsAtLeast
values.comment:
If the `Int ≥` a given `minimum`,
return `Ok` with the `N (Min minimum)`,
else `Err` with the input `Int`.

    4 |> N.intIsAtLeast n5
    --: Result Int (N (Min N5))
    --> Err 4

    1234 |> N.intIsAtLeast n5 |> Result.map N.toInt
    --> Ok 1234
values.type: N.N (N.In min max_ minimumLimitDifference_) -> Basics.Int -> Result.Result Basics.Int (N.N (N.Min min))

values.name: intIsIn
values.comment:
Compared to a range from a lower to an upper bound, is the `Int` in range, [`BelowOrAbove`](#BelowOrAbove)?

    inputIntJudge : Int -> Result String (N (In N1 (Add10 a_) {}))
    inputIntJudge int =
        case int |> N.intIsIn ( n1, n10 ) of
            Ok inRange ->
                inRange |> N.maxOpen n10 |> Ok
            Err (N.Below _) ->
                Err "must be ≥ 1"
            Err (N.Above _) ->
                Err "must be ≤ 100"

    0 |> inputIntJudge
    --> Err "must be ≥ 1"
values.type: ( N.N (N.In minDownLimit maxUpperLimit lowerLimitDifference_), N.N (N.In maxUpperLimit maxUpperLimitAtLeast upperLimitDifference_) ) -> Basics.Int -> Result.Result (N.BelowOrAbove Basics.Int (N.N (N.Min (N.Add1 maxUpperLimit)))) (N.N (N.In minDownLimit maxUpperLimitAtLeast {}))

values.name: is
values.comment:
Is the [`N`](#N) equal to, [`BelowOrAbove`](#BelowOrAbove) a given number?

    giveAPresent { age } =
        case age |> N.is n18 of
            Err (N.Below younger) ->
                toy { age = younger }

            Err (N.Above older) ->
                book { age = older }

            Ok _ ->
                bigPresent

    toy : { age : N (In min_ N17 difference_) } -> Toy

    book :
        { age : N (In (N19 minMinus19_) max_ difference_) }
        -> Book

(The type doesn't forbid that the number you're comparing against
is below the current minimum or above the current maximum.
→ `Err` or `Ok` values don't necessarily follow `min <= max` for `N (In min max ...)`
Luckily that's not a problem, since the values won't be produced anyway.)
values.type: N.N (N.In comparedAgainstMin (N.Add1 comparedAgainstMaxMinus1) comparedAgainstDifference) -> N.N (N.In min max difference_) -> Result.Result (N.BelowOrAbove (N.N (N.In min comparedAgainstMaxMinus1 {})) (N.N (N.In (N.Add1 comparedAgainstMin) max {}))) (N.N (N.In comparedAgainstMin (N.Add1 comparedAgainstMaxMinus1) comparedAgainstDifference))

values.name: isAtLeast
values.comment:
Is the [`N`](#N) below than or at least as big as a given number?

    vote :
        { age : N (In (Add18 minMinus18_) max_ difference_) }
        -> Vote

    tryToVote { age } =
        case age |> N.isAtLeast n18 of
            Err _ ->
                --😓
                Nothing

            Ok oldEnough ->
                vote { age = oldEnough } |> Just

    factorial : N (In min_ max_ difference_) -> N (Min N1)
    factorial =
        factorialBody

    factorialBody : N (In min_ max_ difference_) -> N (Min N1)
    factorialBody x =
        case x |> N.isAtLeast n1 of
            Err _ ->
                n1 |> N.noMax

            Ok atLeast1 ->
                factorial (atLeast1 |> N.minSub n1)
                    |> N.mul atLeast1

(The type doesn't forbid that the lower limit you're comparing against
is below the current minimum or above the current maximum.
→ `Err` or `Ok` values don't necessarily follow `min <= max` for `N (In min max ...)`
Luckily that's not a problem, since the values won't be produced anyway.)
values.type: N.N (N.In lowerLimitMin (N.Add1 lowerLimitMaxMinus1) lowerLimitDifference_) -> N.N (N.In min max difference_) -> Result.Result (N.N (N.In min lowerLimitMaxMinus1 {})) (N.N (N.In lowerLimitMin max {}))

values.name: isAtMost
values.comment:
Is the [`N`](#N) at most (`Ok`) or greater than (`Err`) a given number?

    goToBelow18Party : { age : N (In min_ N17 difference_) } -> Snack

    tryToGoToBelow18Party { age } =
        case age |> N.isAtMost n17 of
            Ok below18 ->
                goToBelow18Party { age = below18 } |> Just

            Err _ ->
                Nothing

(The type doesn't forbid that the upper limit you're comparing against
is below the current minimum or above the current maximum.
→ `Err` or `Ok` values don't necessarily follow `min <= max` for `N (In min max ...)`
Luckily that's not a problem, since the values won't be produced anyway.)
values.type: N.N (N.In upperLimitMin upperLimitMax upperLimitDifference_) -> N.N (N.In min max difference_) -> Result.Result (N.N (N.In (N.Add1 upperLimitMin) max {})) (N.N (N.In min upperLimitMax {}))

values.name: isIn
values.comment:
Compared to a range from a lower to an upper bound,
is the [`N`](#N) in range or [`BelowOrAbove`](#BelowOrAbove)?

    isIn3To10 : N (In min_ (Add10 maxMinus10_) d_) -> Maybe (N (In N3 (Add10 a_) {}))
    isIn3To10 =
        N.isIn ( n3, n10 )
            >> Result.toMaybe

    n9 |> isIn3To10 |> Maybe.map N.toInt
    --> Just 9

    n12 |> isIn3To10
    --> Nothing

(The type doesn't forbid that the limits you're comparing against
are below the current minimum, above the current maximum or in the wrong order.
→ `Err` or `Ok` values don't necessarily follow `min <= max` for `N (In min max ...)`
Luckily that's not a problem, since the values won't be produced anyway.)

Here's some example-cases:

  - `hi < lo < min < max` → `Below | Above`
  - `hi < min < lo < max` → `Below | Above`
  - `hi < min < max < lo` → `Below | Above`
  - `lo < hi < min < max` → `Above`
  - `max < hi < min < lo` → `Below`
  - `min < hi < lo < max` → `Below | Above`
  - `lo < min < hi < max` → `Ok | Above`
  - `max < min < hi < lo` → `Below`
  - `max < lo < hi < min` → `Below | Ok | Above` ← the default case
  - `lo < min < max < hi` → `Ok`
  - `min < lo < max < hi` → `Ok | Below`
  - `min < max < lo < hi` → `Below`

.

  - did we miss something? → issue
  - feel motivated to add the other cases? → PR
values.type: ( N.N (N.In lowerLimit (N.Add1 lowerLimitMaxMinus1) lowerLimitDifference_), N.N (N.In upperLimitMin upperLimitMax upperLimitDifference_) ) -> N.N (N.In min max difference_) -> Result.Result (N.BelowOrAbove (N.N (N.In min lowerLimitMaxMinus1 {})) (N.N (N.In (N.Add1 upperLimitMin) max {}))) (N.N (N.In lowerLimit upperLimitMax {}))

values.name: maxOpen
values.comment:
Make it fit into functions with require a higher maximum.

You should type arguments and stored types as broad as possible.

    onlyAtMost18 : N (In min_ N18 difference_) -> ...

    onlyAtMost18 between3And8 -- works

But once you implement `onlyAtMost18`, you might use the n in `onlyAtMost19`:

    onlyAtMost18 n =
        -- onlyAtMost19 n → error
        onlyAtMost19 (n |> N.maxOpen n18)
values.type: N.N (N.In max maxOpen maxOpenDifference_) -> N.N (N.In min max difference_) -> N.N (N.In min maxOpen {})

values.name: maxUp
values.comment:
Have a specific maximum in mind? → [`maxOpen`](#maxOpen)

Want to increase the upper bound by a fixed amount? ↓

    maxUp4 : N (In min max difference_) -> N (In min (Add4 max) {})
    maxUp4 =
        N.maxUp n4

When is this useful? Very rarely. This is how [`up`](#up) reverse could be implemented

    down :
        N (In min (Add1 maxMinus1) difference_)
        -> List (N (In N0 maxMinus1 {}))
    down length =
        case length |> isAtLeast n1 of
            Err _ ->
                []

            Ok lengthAtLeast1 ->
                let
                    lengthMinus1 =
                        lengthAtLeast1 |> sub n1
                in
                lengthMinus1
                    :: (lengthMinus1
                            -- → In N0 maxMinus1
                            |> maxUp n1
                            |> downRecursive
                       )

(to be fair: here, you could also use `lengthAtLeast1 |> subMin n1` instead of `lengthMinus1 |> maxUp n1`)
values.type: N.N (N.In increase_ increaseAtLeast_ (N.Is (N.Diff max N.To maxIncreased) diff1_)) -> N.N (N.In min max difference_) -> N.N (N.In min maxIncreased {})

values.name: maximumUnknown
values.comment: Constructor for [`NoMax`](#NoMax)
values.type: N.NoMax

values.name: minAdd
values.comment:
To the [`N`](#N) without a known maximum-constraint,
add a specific [`N (In ... (Is ...))`](#Is) value.

    atLeast70 |> N.add n7
    --: N (Min N77)

Use [`addAtLeast`](#addAtLeast) if you want to add an [`N`](#N) that can be in a range.
values.type: N.N (N.In added_ addedAtLeast_ (N.Is (N.Diff min N.To sumMin) addedDiff1_)) -> N.N (N.In min max_ difference_) -> N.N (N.Min sumMin)

values.name: minDown
values.comment:
Set the minimum lower.

    [ atLeast3, atLeast4 ]

Elm complains:

> But all the previous elements in the list are: `N (Min N3)`

    [ atLeast3
    , atLeast4 |> N.minDown n3
    ]
values.type: N.N (N.In newMin min lowerDifference_) -> N.N (N.In min max difference_) -> N.N (N.In newMin max {})

values.name: minSub
values.comment:
From an [`N`](#N) with an unknown maximum constraint,
subtract a [specific number](#Is)

    atLeast7 |> N.minSub n2
    --: N (Min N5)

Use [`minSubAtMost`](#minSubAtMost) if you want to subtract an [`N`](#N) that can be in a range.
values.type: N.N (N.In subtracted_ subtractedAtLeast_ (N.Is (N.Diff differenceMin N.To min) subtractedDiff1_)) -> N.N (N.In min max difference_) -> N.N (N.In differenceMin max {})

values.name: minSubAtMost
values.comment:
From an [`N`](#N) without an unknown maximum constraint,
subtract a number in a range.

The first argument is the maximum of the subtracted number.

    atLeast6 |> N.minSubAtMost n5 between0And5
    --: N (Min N1)
values.type: N.N (N.In subtractedMax subtractedMaxAtLeast_ (N.Is (N.Diff differenceMin N.To min) subtractedMaxDiff1_)) -> N.N (N.In (N.N0able subtractedMinPlus1_ Possibly.Possibly) subtractedMax subtractedDifference_) -> N.N (N.In min max difference_) -> N.N (N.In differenceMin max {})

values.name: minimum
values.comment: The smallest allowed number promised by the [range type](#In).
values.type: N.N (N.In minimum maximum_ difference_) -> minimum

values.name: mul
values.comment:
Multiply by [`N`](#N) `n ≥ 1`.
we know that if `n ≥ 1`, `x * n ≥ x`.

    atLeast5 |> N.mul n2
    --: N (Min N5)

    atLeast2 |> N.mul n5
    --: N (Min N2)
values.type: N.N (N.In (N.Add1 multiplicandMinMinus1_) multiplicandMax_ multiplicandDifference_) -> N.N (N.In min max_ difference_) -> N.N (N.Min min)

values.name: n0
values.comment: The exact natural number `0`
values.type: N.N (N.In N.N0 atLeast_ (N.Is (N.Diff x0 N.To x0) (N.Diff x1 N.To x1)))

values.name: n1
values.comment: The exact natural number `1`
values.type: N.N (N.In N.N1 (N.Add1 atLeast_) (N.Is (N.Diff x0 N.To (N.Add1 x0)) (N.Diff x1 N.To (N.Add1 x1))))

values.name: n10
values.comment: The exact natural number `10`
values.type: N.N (N.In N.N10 (N.Add10 atLeast_) (N.Is (N.Diff x0 N.To (N.Add10 x0)) (N.Diff x1 N.To (N.Add10 x1))))

values.name: n11
values.comment: The exact natural number `11`
values.type: N.N (N.In N.N11 (N.Add11 atLeast_) (N.Is (N.Diff x0 N.To (N.Add11 x0)) (N.Diff x1 N.To (N.Add11 x1))))

values.name: n12
values.comment: The exact natural number `12`
values.type: N.N (N.In N.N12 (N.Add12 atLeast_) (N.Is (N.Diff x0 N.To (N.Add12 x0)) (N.Diff x1 N.To (N.Add12 x1))))

values.name: n13
values.comment: The exact natural number `13`
values.type: N.N (N.In N.N13 (N.Add13 atLeast_) (N.Is (N.Diff x0 N.To (N.Add13 x0)) (N.Diff x1 N.To (N.Add13 x1))))

values.name: n14
values.comment: The exact natural number `14`
values.type: N.N (N.In N.N14 (N.Add14 atLeast_) (N.Is (N.Diff x0 N.To (N.Add14 x0)) (N.Diff x1 N.To (N.Add14 x1))))

values.name: n15
values.comment: The exact natural number `15`
values.type: N.N (N.In N.N15 (N.Add15 atLeast_) (N.Is (N.Diff x0 N.To (N.Add15 x0)) (N.Diff x1 N.To (N.Add15 x1))))

values.name: n16
values.comment: The exact natural number `16`
values.type: N.N (N.In N.N16 (N.Add16 atLeast_) (N.Is (N.Diff x0 N.To (N.Add16 x0)) (N.Diff x1 N.To (N.Add16 x1))))

values.name: n2
values.comment: The exact natural number `2`
values.type: N.N (N.In N.N2 (N.Add2 atLeast_) (N.Is (N.Diff x0 N.To (N.Add2 x0)) (N.Diff x1 N.To (N.Add2 x1))))

values.name: n3
values.comment: The exact natural number `3`
values.type: N.N (N.In N.N3 (N.Add3 atLeast_) (N.Is (N.Diff x0 N.To (N.Add3 x0)) (N.Diff x1 N.To (N.Add3 x1))))

values.name: n4
values.comment: The exact natural number `4`
values.type: N.N (N.In N.N4 (N.Add4 atLeast_) (N.Is (N.Diff x0 N.To (N.Add4 x0)) (N.Diff x1 N.To (N.Add4 x1))))

values.name: n5
values.comment: The exact natural number `5`
values.type: N.N (N.In N.N5 (N.Add5 atLeast_) (N.Is (N.Diff x0 N.To (N.Add5 x0)) (N.Diff x1 N.To (N.Add5 x1))))

values.name: n6
values.comment: The exact natural number `6`
values.type: N.N (N.In N.N6 (N.Add6 atLeast_) (N.Is (N.Diff x0 N.To (N.Add6 x0)) (N.Diff x1 N.To (N.Add6 x1))))

values.name: n7
values.comment: The exact natural number `7`
values.type: N.N (N.In N.N7 (N.Add7 atLeast_) (N.Is (N.Diff x0 N.To (N.Add7 x0)) (N.Diff x1 N.To (N.Add7 x1))))

values.name: n8
values.comment: The exact natural number `8`
values.type: N.N (N.In N.N8 (N.Add8 atLeast_) (N.Is (N.Diff x0 N.To (N.Add8 x0)) (N.Diff x1 N.To (N.Add8 x1))))

values.name: n9
values.comment: The exact natural number `9`
values.type: N.N (N.In N.N9 (N.Add9 atLeast_) (N.Is (N.Diff x0 N.To (N.Add9 x0)) (N.Diff x1 N.To (N.Add9 x1))))

values.name: noDiff
values.comment:
Convert it to a `N (In min max {})`.

    n4 |> N.noDiff
    --: N (In N4 (Add4 a_) {})

Example

    [ in3To10, n3 ]

> all the previous elements in the list are: `N (In N3 N10 {})`

    [ in3To10
    , n3 |> N.noDiff
    ]
values.type: N.N (N.In min max difference_) -> N.N (N.In min max {})

values.name: noMax
values.comment:
Convert a `N (In min ...)` to a `N (Min min)`.

    between3And10 |> N.noMax
    --: N (Min N4)

Use it to unify different types of number minimum constraints like

    [ atLeast1, between1And10 ]

Elm complains:

> But all the previous elements in the list are: `N (Min N1)`

    [ atLeast1
    , between1And10 |> N.noMax
    ]
values.type: N.N (N.In min max_ difference_) -> N.N (N.Min min)

values.name: random
values.comment:
Generate a random [`N`](#N) in a range.

    N.random ( n1, n10 )
    --: Random.Generator (N (In N1 (Add10 a_) {}))
values.type: ( N.N (N.In lowerLimitMin upperLimitMin lowerLimitDifference_), N.N (N.In upperLimitMin upperLimitMax upperLimitDifference_) ) -> Random.Generator (N.N (N.In lowerLimitMin upperLimitMax {}))

values.name: remainderBy
values.comment:
The remainder after dividing by a [`N`](#N) `d ≥ 1`.
We know `x % d ≤ d - 1`

    atMost7 |> N.remainderBy n3
    --: N (In N0 (Add3 a_) {})
values.type: N.N (N.In (N.Add1 divisorMinMinus1_) (N.Add1 divisorMaxMinus1) divisorDifference_) -> N.N (N.In min_ max_ difference_) -> N.N (N.In N.N0 divisorMaxMinus1 {})

values.name: sub
values.comment:
Subtract a given specific [`N (In ... (Is ...))`](#Is).

    between7And10 |> N.sub n7
    --: N (In N0 (Add3 a_) {})

Use [`subIn`](#subIn) if you want to subtract an [`N`](#N) that isn't a [`N (In ... (Is ...))`](#Is).
values.type: N.N (N.In subtractedMin_ subtractedMax_ (N.Is (N.Diff differenceMin N.To min) (N.Diff differenceMax N.To max))) -> N.N (N.In min max diff_) -> N.N (N.In differenceMin differenceMax {})

values.name: subDifference
values.comment:
To the number, subtract a specific other one by supplying one of its [difference](#Diff)s
promised by its type [`Is (Diff low0 To high0) (Diff low1 To high1)`](#Is)
values.type: N.Diff low N.To high -> high -> low

values.name: subIn
values.comment:
Subtract an [`N`](#N) that can be in a range.

The tuple argument should contain

1.  the minimum subtracted value
2.  the maximum subtracted value

```
between6And12 |> N.subIn ( n1, n5 ) between1And5
--: N (In N1 (Add5 a_) {})
```
values.type: ( N.N (N.In subtractedMin subtractedMinAtLeast_ (N.Is (N.Diff differenceMax N.To max) subtractedMinDiff1_)), N.N (N.In subtractedMax subtractedMaxAtLeast_ (N.Is (N.Diff differenceMin N.To min) subtractedMaxDiff1_)) ) -> N.N (N.In subtractedMin subtractedMax subtractedDifference_) -> N.N (N.In min max difference_) -> N.N (N.In differenceMin differenceMax {})

values.name: toFloat
values.comment:
Drop the range constraints
to feed another library with its `Float` representation.
values.type: N.N range_ -> Basics.Float

values.name: toInt
values.comment:
Drop the range constraints
to feed another library with its `Int` representation.
values.type: N.N range_ -> Basics.Int

values.name: toPower
values.comment:
[`N`](#N) Raised to a given power `p ≥ 1`
→ `x ^ p ≥ x`

    atLeast5 |> N.toPower n2
    --: N (Min N5)

    atLeast2 |> N.toPower n5
    --: N (Min N2)
values.type: N.N (N.In (N.Add1 exponentMinMinus1_) (N.Add1 exponentMax_) exponentDifference_) -> N.N (N.In min max_ difference_) -> N.N (N.Min min)

values.name: until
values.comment:
[`N`](#N)s increasing from `0` to `n`
In the end, there are `n` numbers.

    N.until n6
        |> List.map (N.add n3)
        --: List (N (In N3 (Add9 a_)))
        |> List.map N.toInt
    --> [ 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9 ]

    N.up atLeast7 |> List.map (N.add n3)
    --: List (N (Min N10))

[`typesafe-array`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-typesafe-array/latest/Arr#nats) even knows the length! Try it.
values.type: N.N (N.In min_ max difference_) -> Emptiable.Emptiable (Stack.Stacked (N.N (N.In N.N0 max {}))) Basics.Never

values.name: up
values.comment:
[`N`](#N)s increasing from `0` to `n - 1`.
In the end, there are `n` numbers.

> @deprecated in favor of [`until`](#until)
> If you'd like to keep this → issue

    N.up n7
        |> List.map (N.add n3)
        --: List (N (In N3 (Add9 a_)))
        |> List.map N.toInt
    --> [ 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9 ]

[`typesafe-array`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-typesafe-array/latest/Arr#nats) even knows the length! Try it.
values.type: N.N (N.In min_ (N.Add1 maxMinus1) difference_) -> List.List (N.N (N.In N.N0 maxMinus1 {}))
